zh.lifeSummary: 當Herbert Saint Quintin III於1286年出生在英國多瑟特時，他的父親Herbert de St Quintin V24歲，母親Anastasia Maltravers22歲。他於大約1311年與Lora de Fauconberge在英國多瑟特福洛 聖 昆丁結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1339年2月7日在英國越可爾布藍德斯波頓享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Herbert Saint Quintin III于1286年出生在英国多瑟特时，他的父亲Herbert de St Quintin V24岁，母亲Anastasia Maltravers22岁。他于大约1311年与Lora de Fauconberge在英国多瑟特福洛 聖 昆丁结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1339年2月7日在英国越可爾布藍德斯波頓享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Quintin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Yves, Andre, Dominique, Gilles, Lucien, Michel, Oliva, Roch.
Breton: from the personal name Quintin, which is of the same Latin origin as 2 below, but also considered a derivative of Old Breton kint ‘first’.
French; Spanish (Quintín): from the personal name Quintin (Spanish Quintín), from Latin Quintinus, a Christian saint's name (see Quentin ).

variants.name: Quinton
meanings: English: from the Old French and Middle English personal name Quentin, Quintin (Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, and brought to England by the Normans. The surname was altered to Quinton by association with the placenames in 2 below.

English: habitational name from from any of three places called Quinton (Gloucestershire, Northamptonshire, Worcestershire) or from Quainton (Buckinghamshire). The placenames probably derive from Old English cwēn ‘queen’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, or perhaps from Old English cwene ‘woman’ + tūn or an Old English personal name Cwēna + Old English connective -ing- + tūn.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Quentin (Manche) in France or possibly from Saint-Quentin-en-Tourmont (Somme), the site of the martyrdom of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. It is not certain that this surname survived into the modern period.

variants.name: Quentin
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Quentin (from Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. Compare Quintin .
English: variant of   Quinton  .

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

variants.name: Heribert
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Herbert Saint Quintin III was born in 1286, in Dorset, England, his father, Herbert de St Quintin V, was 24 and his mother, Anastasia Maltravers, was 22. He married Lora de Fauconberge about 1311, in Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 7 February 1339, in Brandesburton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 53.

fr.Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England, Great: Frome St Quintin, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Herbert Saint Quintin III est né en 1286, à Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Herbert de St Quintin V, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Anastasia Maltravers, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Lora de Fauconberge environ 1311, à Frome St Quintin, Dorset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 7 février 1339, à Brandesburton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England, Great: Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Herbert Saint Quintin III 1286 in Dorset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Herbert de St Quintin V 24 und seine Mutter Anastasia Maltravers 22. Er hat um 1311 in Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England Lora de Fauconberge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 7. Februar 1339 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Brandesburton, Yorkshire, England.

Lui sposato Lora de Fauconberge circa 1311, nel Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 7 febbraio 1339, nel Brandesburton, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 53 anni.

ko.Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England, Great: 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 프롬 스트 퀸틴
ko.lifeSummary: Herbert Saint Quintin III(이)가 1286년 영국 도르세트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herbert de St Quintin V(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Anastasia Maltravers(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1311년 영국 도르세트 프롬 스트 퀸틴에서 Lora de Fauconberge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1339년 2월 7일 영국 요르크실르 브랜데스버턴에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England, Great: Frome St Quintin, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Herbert Saint Quintin III nasceu em 1286, em Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Herbert de St Quintin V, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Anastasia Maltravers, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Lora de Fauconberge aproximadamente 1311, em Frome St Quintin, Dorset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 7 de fevereiro de 1339, em Brandesburton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 53 anos.

ru.Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England, Great: фром ст куинтинь, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Herbert Saint Quintin III родился в 1286 г. в местности дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Herbert de St Quintin V было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Anastasia Maltravers, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Lora de Fauconberge около 1311 г. в местности фром ст куинтинь, дорсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 7 февраля 1339 г. в местности брэндесбуртон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 53 лет.
